title: The Cleansing of the Temple
subtitle: The Lord enters His Father's house and drives out the buyers and sellers a second time

stillness: Be still, and enter first the house of prayer.
Wherever you go, come first to the place where God is found.
Commend yourself to God in prayer, and only then turn to your affairs.
gospel: Jesus came directly to the temple,
and entered into the very temple of God.
Seeing in His Father's house this trafficking, this den of thieves,
stirred by the ardour of the Spirit,
He made Himself a scourge of little cords,
and the Lord of the temple drove out all who sold and bought in the temple,
He overturned the tables of the money-changers,
and the seats of those who sold doves.
Nor would He suffer any vessel not dedicated and consecrated to God
to be carried through the temple for the sake of trade.
behold: He came straight to the temple, before all else,
for a public place it was, where He might be found,
showing that He suffered not unwillingly, but of His own accord.
He entered to show that the ruin of the city and the people, for which He had wept,
came chiefly from the fault of the priests,
and that from there the root of perdition went forth.
Watch Him enter, where He sets forth the sign of His coming judgment.
consider: As the temple is in the city, so is the life of the priests among the people.
The simony of the priests was the cause of the destruction of the Jews.
The priests, consulting their own greed, sold victims of every kind in the porches and courts,
and set money-changers there, who lent money under pledge and in hope of some gift.
What the Law forbade in coin, they made lawful in things that coin can buy.
And so today many are like them, cloaking usury and simony under the name of charity, or of gratitude.
It is a perilous thing to take gifts, for by this a man sells his liberty.
Some things seem given freely, which cost us most dearly: we ourselves are the price.
To accept a benefit is to sell one's freedom.
The heart and the stomach are understood as the priesthood, for by them the whole people is governed in spiritual things.
As the heart is the seat of wisdom, so the priests are the vessels of spiritual wisdom.
If the priesthood be whole, the whole Church flowers; if it be corrupt, the faith is withered.
If one of the Christians sin, not all the priests sin; but if the priests be in sin, the whole people is turned to sinning.
When you see a tree pale and withered in its leaves, you know it has some hurt at the root.
So when you see a people undisciplined and irreligious, know without doubt that its priesthood is not sound.
Greatly, then, are the clergy to be wept over when they fall, for their fall is the more perilous.
Nothing so destroys the Church of God as for the clergy to be worse than the laity.
prayer: Lord Jesus, Lord of the temple,
who came straight to Your Father's house and drove out all that defiled it,
made for Yourself a scourge of little cords and overturned the tables of greed,
come into the temple of my heart;
drive out from me all trafficking, all usury, all that is not consecrated to You,
that nothing be carried through me for the sake of gain,
but that I may be a vessel dedicated to God,
and my soul made again a house of prayer.

latinJewel.latin: Sicut enim templum est in civitate, ita vita sacerdotum in plebe.
latinJewel.english: For as the temple stands in the city, so stands the life of the priests among the people.
